place: Estell Manor city, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: In 1832, Joseph West built a sandstone manor house for his sister Maria West and her husband Daniel Estell, giving the town its name.
During World War I, the U.S. government selected part of Estell Manor in 1918 for the Bethlehem Loading Company munitions plant.
Today, Estell Manor Park is managed by Atlantic County’s Division of Parks and Recreation, which maintains trails, picnic areas and a Nature Center.
Estell Manor Park ranks among Atlantic County’s most significant historic and recreational sites, blending natural beauty with industrial heritage.
Fox Nature Center houses the ruins of a glassmaking village, a World War I munitions plant, and several historic cemeteries.
